assumptions: Modelled the 528-byte rbp-anchored region as a single st_parameter_dt struct with named common/filename/line fields; the remaining bytes are kept as opaque padding because the binary only writes those three slots before each I/O burst.
Kept 0x600000080 as a single 64-bit 'common' store rather than splitting into the documented unit/flags 32-bit halves, matching the binary's single qword move.

Folded the unrolled first iteration of the argument loop (the block before L_1290 plus the L_1290 self-loop) into a single `for (i = 1; i <= num_args; ++i)`. The slt/sle compares against num_args drove the bound and direction.
Treated arg_index (stack slot at rsp+4) as the loop induction variable passed by reference to get_command_argument; the binary reloads stack_5 each iteration which matches passing &arg_index.

Treated stack_3 as `total_len` (printed for 'Total argument length:') and the outer iargc() return as `num_args` (printed for 'Number of arguments:'); rsp+8 / rsp+12 stack slots were the addresses transferred to gfortran for those PRINTs.
arg_buf size set to 100 from the buflen argument passed to _gfortran_get_command_argument_i4 / _gfortran_string_len_trim.
`&[var7+0x4014]` rewritten as an extern int32_t `global_counter` (BSS); the +1 store before the line-40 PRINT was kept as the inlined subroutine body.

`call_count.1` rewritten as extern `call_count_1` since C identifiers cannot contain '.'; the increment was inlined at the call site by the compiler so it is reproduced here verbatim rather than wrapped in a fabricated function.
Dropped optimizer-only stack reload chains (stack_4/stack_6 round-trips) and the redundant double assignment of stack_5 inside the loop epilogue.
Source filename string (`/workspace/source/fortran/hello.f90`) hoisted to a single SRC_FILE constant — the binary references it from every descriptor setup.
Line numbers 12, 25, 26, 27, 40, 41 are taken verbatim from the descriptor stores; they are not inferred.
divergences: [low] other: LLM unavailable; equivalence could not be checked
